name: markitdown-attachments
description: Convert Claude chat & project attachments to Markdown files locally — with OCR — without spending context tokens on the file contents.
long_description:
Wraps Microsoft's MarkItDown as an MCP server, but unlike the stock `markitdown-mcp` it never returns converted text into the conversation. Each attachment (PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, images, HTML, CSV, JSON, XML, ZIP, EPub, Outlook .msg, audio, and more) is converted entirely on your machine and written to a `.md` file on disk; the tools return only compact metadata (output paths, byte/character counts, status). Converting a whole folder of attachments costs effectively zero context tokens.

Features: OCR (Tesseract) for images and scanned/image-only PDFs with automatic orientation detection, including a 'hybrid' mode that keeps text pages and OCRs only the image pages of mixed PDFs; existing .md/.markdown files are carried through into the output so a folder sweep yields a complete collection; Google Drive pointer files (.gdoc/.gslides/.gsheet/.gdrive) become clickable 'Open in Drive' link notes (no raw JSON / no embedded email); collision-safe output names (name.pdf.md / name.docx.md); structure-preserving output; a 'detail=summary' option to shrink the tool result on very large sweeps (full manifest still written to INDEX.md); and a rich, still-token-free summary.

Tools: convert_attachments_to_markdown, list_convertible_attachments, convert_one, peek_markdown, ocr_capabilities.

tools.name: convert_attachments_to_markdown
tools.description: Batch-convert files, directories, and globs to Markdown FILES on disk (with optional OCR). Returns only metadata — token-free.

tools.name: list_convertible_attachments
tools.description: List convertible files in a directory (paths/sizes only); Google Drive pointer stubs reported separately.

tools.name: convert_one
tools.description: Convert a single file to a Markdown file on disk; returns only metadata.

tools.name: peek_markdown
tools.description: Opt-in: return a small, capped preview (default 400 chars) of a generated Markdown file.

tools.name: ocr_capabilities
tools.description: Report whether Tesseract OCR is available, its path, version, and installed languages.

input_directory.title: Default attachments folder
input_directory.description: Optional. Folder scanned when you ask to convert attachments without naming specific files.

output_directory.title: Markdown output folder
output_directory.description: Optional. Where generated .md files are written. If empty, each .md is written next to its source file.

ocr_mode.title: OCR mode
ocr_mode.description: auto = OCR images & image-only PDFs (default); off = never; force = OCR every image/PDF; hybrid = per-page (OCR only the image pages of mixed PDFs). Requires Tesseract.

ocr_lang.title: OCR language(s)
ocr_lang.description: Tesseract language code(s), e.g. 'eng' or 'eng+ben'. Install extra languages with: brew install tesseract-lang.

enable_plugins.title: Enable MarkItDown plugins
enable_plugins.description: Allow third-party markitdown plugins (off by default).
